results.possibleIdentity: Jeffrey N. Bairstow was a computer engineer, mathematician, and influential technical journalist who was active in the professional technology and engineering communities of New York City starting in the late 1960s. He served as a Technical Editor for 'Electronic Design' magazine in 1967 and was a founding editor and later managing editor of 'Computer Decisions' magazine around 1969–1970. His background as a computer engineer and mathematician, combined with his location in New York and his role at 'Electronic Design,' placed him within the network of technical professionals who interacted with and reported on the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) organization during its peak years of promoting collaborations between artists and engineers. In the 1980s, Bairstow became a senior editor for 'High Technology' magazine, where he specialized in emerging technologies such as computer-aided engineering (CAE), application-specific integrated circuits (ASICs), and CD-ROM technology. His career continued well into the 2000s as a contributing editor and columnist for 'Laser Focus World,' where he wrote the 'In My View' column. In addition to his technical journalism, he co-authored several diverse titles, including 'Tennis Begins at 40' (1976) with tennis legend Pancho Gonzalez and 'I.T., Interval Training for Lifetime Fitness' (1980).
